body.title: Establish slow-metrics-to-SensorTree pattern; migrate 2-3 examples
body.result: Rule theigors/rules/metrics established: slow progress metrics live in palace subtrees under theigors/metrics/. Frequency heuristic documented. Two example subtrees created: pass2_findings (Pass-2 epic progress, seeded 6/6/148) and habit_health (day-close-audit placeholder). Backing helpers already in wild_igor/igor/tools/palace_metric.py from T-approach-frame-sensor-node.
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-21: 'would it make sense to do all our metric recording that way?' Yes for slow/progress-shaped metrics. Rule: if a human would care to read this number once a week, put it in SensorTree with versioned memory. High-frequency instrumentation stays in existing stores.

**Task:**
1. Write rule as palace node at `theigors/rules/metrics` (3-4 lines: the rule + the frequency heuristic + pointer to T-approach-frame-sensor-node as the reference implementation).
2. Identify 2-3 existing slow metrics to migrate. Candidates: Pass-2 findings resolved count, OR burn-rate gauge, habit health score from day-close-audit.
3. Migrate those 2-3 using T-approach-frame-sensor-node as the template.
4. Document the pattern in the palace node.

**Affected files:** palace node `theigors/rules/metrics` (new); 2-3 new `theigors/metrics/<name>/` subtrees (clan.memory_palace); possibly minor edits to the scripts that emit the chosen metrics, to also write to SensorTree — update those files' docstrings if touched.

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (Postgres only, clan.memory_palace); no-new-memory-schemas (new paths not new tables); docs-in-code (if any script is touched, update docstring); test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: rule node + 2-3 example migrations.
- Out: migrating ALL existing metrics (use the hook pattern going forward).

**Gate:** T-approach-frame-sensor-node (pattern must exist before this extends it).

**Test plan:** each migrated metric: confirm counter increments, history appends, sparkline renders. Rule node: readable via context-load.
